Between 29 November 2025 and 27 February 2026, 2514 highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) A(H5) virus detections were reported in domestic (406) and wild (2108) birds in 32 countries in Europe.
Waterfowl species were affected to a greater extent than in previous years, and more than 90% of detections in poultry were due to primary introduction from wild birds.
High viral circulation in wild birds resulted in a slight increase in detections in mammals in Europe, with the first potential spillover event from wild birds to dairy cattle suggested in the Netherlands.
